message: MC/TC and EPL-FSI f-I validation targets use different current-injection protocols. Burton & Urban 2014 MC/TC values use 2 s depolarizing steps from 0 to 300 pA in 50 pA increments. Burton, Malyshko & Urban 2024 EPL-FSI values use 500 ms depolarizing steps from 50 to 600 pA in 50 pA increments, with firing rate computed from median inverse ISI. Compare model outputs only against rows with matching protocol_id unless an explicit normalization/reanalysis step is implemented.

Some positive-only Burton & Urban 2014 metrics in this audit use audit-reconstructed non-symmetric acceptance bands such as lognormal_sd. These bands preserve the paper's reported arithmetic mean and standard deviation but do not imply that Burton & Urban modeled the underlying distributions as lognormal. Treat the band shape as an audit-side comparison assumption, not as a source-reported statistical model.
source_location: Legacy MC/TC reference CSV plus validation-layer band selection

message: The Burton & Urban 2014 afterhyperpolarization-duration acceptance band is currently treated as a provisional positive-only reconstruction. The source rows provide arithmetic mean and standard deviation, but there is not yet a source-backed quantile table or raw-cell distribution fit for this metric in the current audit path.

message: Burton, Malyshko & Urban 2024 S8 current-rate rows are example-cell traces rather than population-level averages. Use them as tagged validation targets with sample_scope = example_cell.

PV+, CRH+, and PV/CRH-overlap-adjacent EPL interneuron rows are intentionally kept separate by marker_profile and protocol_id. Do not pool them unless the source explicitly identifies the same population under a compatible protocol.
source: Huang et al. (2013); Kato et al. (2013); Burton, Malyshko & Urban (2024)
